data.title: Gallego: Johnson ‘probably going to lose the Speakership’
data.description:
The Hill

Gallego: Johnson ‘probably going to lose the Speakership’

Ashleigh Fields
October 10, 2025 at 12:17 PM·2 min read

Democratic Sen. Ruben Gallego (Ariz.) said Thursday that Rep. Mike Johnson (R-La.) was likely to lose a chance at another term as House Speaker over his decision not to bring the lower chamber back in session amid the government shutdown.

“Johnson sent his team home, not necessarily just to put pressure on us, but so he doesn‘t get pressure from his own members,” Gallego said during a Thursday appearance on CNN’s “The Source.”

“He‘s barely going to win, and he‘s probably going to lose the Speakership next year. But he‘s putting his members on a plank that‘s not reversible,” he added.

Johnson clinched the Speakership for a second time earlier this year with overwhelming support from President Trump. During his first term with the gavel, he experienced harsh criticism from Reps. Ralph Norman (R-S.C.) and Keith Self (R-Texas).

However, both ultimately voted for him to secure a second term. The only defect came from Rep. Thomas Massie (R-Ky.), who has frequently expressed qualms with Johnson’s leadership style throughout the 119th Congress.

Amid the government shutdown, GOP Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (Ga.) has been a vocal critic of Johnson’s decision not to bring the House back in session and failing to provide a plan for the future of Affordable Care Act subsidies.

“I think he should really bring the House back in session for many reasons,” Greene told CNN in an interview Wednesday, while criticizing Johnson for stalling legislative business.

“Any serious Speaker of the House is going to build consensus within his conference behind a plan. It’s not something secret that gets worked on in a committee,” she said, referring to Republicans’ refusal to negotiate on extending the Affordable Care Act premium health care subsidies in exchange for Democratic votes to reopen the government.

Senate Democrats have been blocking a stopgap measure from advancing in an effort to ensure Republicans back the extension of the Affordable Care Act premium subsidies before open enrollment on Nov. 1.

However, Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) said the topic is not up for discussion until after the federal funding lapse is resolved.

“It is a failure on Thune and Johnson, not necessarily because they‘re shutting down, but also they just didn‘t even bring it up to their membership. They didn‘t bring up, ‘Hey, we need to start negotiating to come up with a path out of this,’” Gallego told anchor Kaitlan Collins on CNN.

Copyright 2025 Nexstar Media, Inc. All rights reserved. This material may not be published, broadcast, rewritten, or redistributed.

For the latest news, weather, sports, and streaming video, head to The Hill.
data.summary: Democratic Sen. Ruben Gallego (Ariz.) said Thursday that Rep. data.title: ESPN's Ryan Clark, Scott Van Pelt apologize for 'SportsCenter' comments about Kyren Lacy's alleged role in fatal car crash
data.description:
Yahoo Sports

ESPN's Ryan Clark, Scott Van Pelt apologize for 'SportsCenter' comments about Kyren Lacy's alleged role in fatal car crash

A day after Clark said Lacy died 'knowing he was innocent,' Louisiana State Police released their explanation of why Lacy had been charged with negligent homicide

Nick Bromberg
·Senior writer
Updated October 9, 2025 at 2:44 PM·4 min read

ESPN’s Ryan Clark apologized Thursday morning on “First Take” for remarks he made about ex-LSU wide receiver Kyren Lacy and Lacy’s alleged role in a fatal December car crash earlier in the week.

“In full transparency y’all, I knew Kyren Lacy personally. But nothing matters to me more than the truth. I always strive to do my best to mix authenticity with the most complete and up-to-date information available,” the former NFL defensive back said. “I failed to do that on Monday night based on the subsequent evidence that has been released by the Louisiana State Police Department.

“I set the highest standards of fairness, and most importantly, righteousness in my work. I didn’t meet that standard.”

"I knew Kyren Lacy personally...it's always important to me that I gather all factual and important information to provide the complete story...and when discussed in real time on Monday evening, I failed to do so. And for that, I apologize." - Ryan Clark pic.twitter.com/QnooNsHGND

— Awful Announcing (@awfulannouncing) October 9, 2025

Clark appeared on “SportsCenter” after the Jaguars’ win over the Chiefs on Monday night and criticized the Louisiana State Police and those who had criticized Lacy. The former Tigers wideout was charged with negligent homicide and other counts after his driving actions had allegedly caused a car crash that killed 78-year-old Herman Hall in Lafourche Parish, Louisiana.

Last week, Lacy’s attorney went on a Louisiana TV station to say that Lacy was over 70 yards behind the crash when it happened and that the vehicle that crashed into Hall’s car was driving very close to the truck ahead of it.

Attorney Matt Ory also released a video that showed a trooper repeatedly telling the driver of the truck to make sure that he wrote down that Lacy’s green Dodge Charger made him hit the brakes.

Lacy, 24, was found dead of an apparent self-inflicted gunshot wound in Harris County, Texas, in April after a police pursuit following an alleged altercation with a family member.

Ory's contention that new evidence calls into question Lacy's role in the crash prompted Clark to go on "SportsCenter" with Scott Van Pelt Monday night where Clark stated "Kyren Lacy was innocent."

Kyren Lacy was supposed to be on a NFL field this weekend. That made it important to say “Kyren Lacy was innocent” on ESPN before the night ended. His name and face were plastered everywhere when he was under investigated. So it’s only right we say it now!

There’s no amount of… pic.twitter.com/P9ddR97iU9

— Ryan Clark (@Realrclark25) October 7, 2025

“Kyren Lacy was accused of something and being investigated for something he didn’t do,” Clark said Monday night. “And he died having to live with the guilt and the consequences of a guilty man knowing he was innocent. It recently was discovered that he was 72 yards away from the crash. That police and state policemen tried to coerce and doctor and use ways to manipulate statements that put this young man behind bars. They changed his joy, they changed his life, and eventually he took his own life.

“This was supposed to be a career but at least he was supposed to be here [in the NFL]. At some point throughout his career I would have expected to say his name. So I’m going to say his name tonight. Kyren Lacy was innocent. Kyren Lacy should be here with us. Nothing will ever repair or replace the pain that his parents have to feel and his loved ones have to feel. But we can say his name. There were so many stations that ran the fact that he was being investigated. So I wanted to make sure here, with [Scott Van Pelt] that he was an innocent man and at least know that he has that as he rests.”

Tuesday morning, hours after Clark’s comments, Louisiana State Police released an 11-minute video explaining why Lacy had been charged. The video included witness statements immediately after the crash saying that Lacy’s driving — he was previously passing cars on a two-lane road in a no-passing zone — was a contributing factor in the crash.

Van Pelt has also apologized for “the incomplete reaction” on Monday night’s “SportsCenter.” Van Pelt had recounted to Clark that he had a "visceral" reaction to Ory’s appearance.

Lacy's death came two days before a grand jury hearing was scheduled regarding his charges from the December crash.
data.summary: A day after Clark said Lacy died 'knowing he was innocent,' Louisiana State Police released their explanation of why Lacy had been charged with negligent homicide.
data.author: Yahoo Sports

data.title: 15 Reasons We Should Be Very Worried When A Woman Goes Silent
data.description:
The Bolde

15 Reasons We Should Be Very Worried When A Woman Goes Silent

Danielle Sachs
October 9, 2025 at 3:15 AM·10 min read

When a woman suddenly goes quiet, it can be unsettling. Silence might seem like a simple lack of words, but it often signals something deeper. It’s not just about what she isn't saying; it’s about the myriad of things she might be processing internally. Sometimes, the silence is a way of protecting herself or reflecting on the situation at hand. Here are 15 reasons why a woman's silence might be cause for concern and what it could mean.

1. She's Processing Her Emotions

When she's quiet, she might be processing a whirlwind of emotions. Silence can be her way of taking a step back to sort through how she feels. According to Dr. Elaine Aron, an expert on sensitivity, processing emotions internally is a characteristic of highly sensitive individuals. They tend to absorb more from their environments, which requires them to spend more time in reflection. This means that her silence could be a sign that she's overwhelmed and needs time to understand her feelings.

In these moments, it's important to give her the space she needs. Attempting to rush her or demand explanations can backfire and add to her stress. Instead, offer a gentle reminder that you're there for her when she's ready to talk. This shows that you respect her process and are supportive rather than intrusive. Patience can be a powerful way to show empathy and understanding.

2. She's Feeling Overwhelmed

A silent woman might be dealing with overwhelming stress or anxiety. When things pile up, it becomes difficult to articulate what's going on. Silence, in this case, is a defense mechanism, giving her a break from the whirlwind inside her head. It's her way of pressing pause on the chaos to regain some sense of control. This type of silence is common when life's responsibilities become too burdensome.

Recognizing this silence involves observing changes in her routine or behavior. Is she retreating from activities she usually enjoys? Has she stopped communicating with those she’s close to? These changes can be indicators of overwhelming pressure. Offering a listening ear or a relaxing day can provide her the support she needs to decompress.

3. She's Contemplating Next Steps

Silence can also mean she's contemplating her next move. Decisions, especially big ones, require introspection and time. According to a study by Dr. Sheena Iyengar, a Columbia Business School professor, thoughtful decision-making often involves periods of reflection and silence. This quiet time allows her to weigh options, consider consequences, and align her actions with her values. Her silence is a sign that she's not taking her decisions lightly.

During this time, it’s crucial to respect her need for solitude. Asking too many questions or pushing for a decision can add unnecessary pressure. Instead, offer encouragement and reassure her that you'll support her choices. This approach fosters trust and shows that you value her autonomy and judgment.

4. She's Protecting Herself

Sometimes silence is a form of self-protection. If she feels vulnerable or threatened, withdrawing into silence can feel safer. It's a way to avoid confrontation or exposure to further hurt. This kind of silence is a protective shield she uses to maintain her emotional well-being. It's important to recognize this as a sign that she might be feeling insecure or unsafe.

Approaching this silence requires sensitivity and care. Rather than probing her for reasons, create an environment where she feels comfortable and secure. Show her through actions that you’re a reliable and safe presence. Over time, this can encourage her to open up when she's ready, knowing that you're a trustworthy confidant.

5. She's Experiencing Inner Conflict

Inner conflict can lead to silence as she wrestles with opposing thoughts or emotions. It might be a clash between what she wants and what she thinks she should do. Dr. Leon Festinger, a psychologist known for his work on cognitive dissonance, explains that this type of internal struggle can cause discomfort, leading to withdrawal and silence. Her quietness is often a sign of this mental tug-of-war as she seeks clarity and resolution.

Understanding her silence in this context means acknowledging the complexity of her thoughts. Avoid jumping to conclusions or offering unsolicited advice, which can complicate matters further. Instead, be patient and let her guide the conversation when she feels ready. This approach shows you respect her process and trust her ability to work through her challenges.

6. She's Feeling Unheard

A woman's silence can signify that she feels unheard or dismissed. When repeated attempts to communicate fall on deaf ears, silence becomes her last resort. It’s a sign that she’s reached a point of frustration or resignation. This silence is not passive-aggressive; it's a reflection of feeling undervalued. Recognizing this type of silence is crucial for maintaining healthy communication.

If you notice this silent treatment, it’s important to reassess how you’ve been listening. Are you truly hearing her words, or just waiting for your turn to speak? Engaging in active listening—where you reflect and clarify what she’s saying—can help bridge the gap. By validating her feelings and opinions, you can help her feel valued and understood, breaking the cycle of silence.

7. She's Avoiding Conflict

Silence can also be a strategic choice to avoid conflict. Some women prefer to stay quiet rather than engage in an argument they feel is unproductive. According to a study by Dr. John Gottman, a leading relationship researcher, avoiding conflict can sometimes be effective in maintaining relationship stability. However, when overused, it might also lead to unresolved issues. Her silence here is a temporary truce in hopes of preventing further discord.

Recognizing this form of silence involves understanding her conflict style. Does she typically shy away from confrontation? If so, gently encouraging open dialogue in a non-confrontational way can help. Suggest a calm discussion when both parties are ready, emphasizing a collaborative approach to problem-solving. This way, you can address underlying issues without escalating tensions.

8. She's Reflecting On The Relationship

When she's silent, she could be reflecting on your relationship. This silence can stem from a desire to evaluate her feelings and the dynamics between you. She might be questioning whether her needs are being met or if the relationship aligns with her long-term goals. This introspection is important for her, and it’s crucial to respect her space during this time. Her silence is a sign that she's taking her commitment seriously.

Encouraging open dialogue about the relationship at the right time can be beneficial. This isn't about pressuring her for answers but rather creating a safe space for honest conversation. Discussing mutual goals and addressing any concerns can strengthen the bond. By showing willingness to work together, you demonstrate that the relationship is a partnership that values both perspectives.

9. She's Experiencing Burnout

Burnout can lead to a profound silence as she struggles to cope with exhaustion. With responsibilities piling up, she might find it challenging to communicate. This silence is not about withdrawal but rather conserving energy at a time when she feels overextended. Recognizing the signs of burnout, such as decreased engagement or irritability, can be crucial. Her silence here is a cry for help, albeit a silent one.

Offering tangible support is key to helping her through this phase. This could mean assisting with tasks or simply giving her time to recharge. Encouraging her to seek professional help if needed can also make a significant difference. By showing understanding and support, you help her navigate through the burnout and find her voice again.

10. She's Afraid Of Being Misunderstood

Fear of being misunderstood can lead to silence as she hesitates to express herself. If past attempts to communicate have led to misinterpretation, she might choose silence over the risk of further confusion. This reluctance can mean she’s treading carefully to avoid miscommunication. Her silence here is a reflection of her desire for clarity and understanding. Recognizing this, it’s important to approach her with an open mind.

Take the time to truly understand her perspective, asking clarifying questions when necessary. Reassure her that you value her thoughts and are committed to understanding her viewpoint. Creating an environment where she feels safe to express herself can slowly dissolve the barriers of silence. By demonstrating patience and empathy, you encourage a more open and honest exchange.

11. She's Seeking Solitude

Silence could be her way of seeking solitude, a crucial element for her personal well-being. In a world that's constantly on, the need for quiet time is often overlooked. This silence is about recharging her mental and emotional batteries. It's not about pushing people away but about reconnecting with herself. Recognizing this need can help you support her better.

Allow her the space to retreat and focus on self-care. This might involve encouraging her to indulge in activities that bring her peace and joy. Respect her boundaries during this time, understanding it's not a reflection of her feelings towards you. By supporting her need for solitude, you show that you respect her individuality and care for her well-being.

12. She's Grappling With Doubt

Doubt can cast a long shadow, leading her into silence as she navigates her uncertainties. Whether it’s self-doubt or uncertainty about a situation, this silence is a way of coping with the internal turmoil. It's a pause to consider her options and find her footing. This kind of silence is a stepping stone towards clarity and confidence. Recognizing this, patience becomes your greatest ally.

Offer reassurance and remind her of her strengths, without applying pressure to resolve her doubts quickly. Being a source of encouragement can help reinforce her self-belief. Encourage her to take her time, letting her know it’s okay to feel unsure sometimes. By standing by her during this period of doubt, you demonstrate your unwavering support and belief in her abilities.

13. She's Waiting For The Right Moment

Sometimes, silence is about waiting for the right moment to speak. Timing can be crucial, especially if she’s contemplating a difficult conversation. This silence is strategic, giving her time to prepare her thoughts and approach. It’s not about avoidance, but about ensuring the message is conveyed effectively. Recognizing this, it's important to allow her the time she needs.

Encourage open communication by letting her know you’re ready to listen when she’s ready to talk. Avoid pressing for immediate answers or jumping to conclusions. Respecting her timing can lead to more meaningful and productive conversations. By showing patience and understanding, you foster a safe environment for honest dialogue.

14. She's Reflecting On Personal Growth

Silence can be a period of reflection on personal growth and development. She might be contemplating her journey and the changes she's experiencing. This silence is introspective, a time for self-discovery and reassessment. It's not about ignoring those around her but focusing inward to better understand herself. Recognizing this means appreciating her need for self-reflection.

Support her by encouraging self-exploration and personal growth activities. Be open to discussing her insights and what she's learning about herself. Show interest in her journey without prying or pushing for details. By respecting her process, you show that you value her growth and are supportive of her path to self-discovery.

15. She's Experiencing Grief Or Loss

Grief and loss can render a person silent as they grapple with intense emotions. This silence is profound, reflecting the depth of their sorrow. It's a period where words might feel inadequate to express the pain. Recognizing this silence involves understanding the stages of grief and offering compassion. Her silence here is a natural part of the healing process.

Provide support by being present and offering a listening ear when she's ready to talk. Avoid trying to fix her pain but rather acknowledge her feelings and offer comfort. Encourage her to seek professional support if she’s open to it. By showing compassion and patience, you provide a safe space for her to begin healing in her own time.
data.summary: When a woman goes silent you should be very worried—here's why.
data.author: The Bolde

data.title: Eagle-Eyed Viewers Spotted Something... Unique In This Trump Photo
data.description:
HuffPost

Eagle-Eyed Viewers Spotted Something... Unique In This Trump Photo

David Moye
October 10, 2025 at 1:14 PM·2 min read

A photo of Donald Trump got a helluva reaction when it was posted to social media on Thursday.

The image, taken by photojournalist Jim Watson, shows the president sitting with Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth discussing the proposed Gaza ceasefire and hostage release deal between Israel and Hamas.

Watson’s camera seemed to be positioned so that a golden bald eagle behind Trump appeared more like devil horns emanating from the president’s skull.

Here’s the original picture:

And here’s a cropped version:

Considering how polarizing Trump’s presidency has been, it’s no surprise that the photo went viral on social media.

It’s also no surprise that many people responded to the picture as a joking confirmation of what they already suspected about the president.

Nothing to see here. Never mind Trump's devil horns. It's totally not a warning from God. 😳👇 pic.twitter.com/YkvJPv8FgR

— Bill Madden (@maddenifico) October 10, 2025

It is a sign. Jesus just told me so in a dream. Beware of trump.

— Covie (@covie_93) October 9, 2025

Biblical.

Just not in the way MAGA expected. https://t.co/gUndTVLB5z

— Adam Cochran (adamscochran.eth) (@adamscochran) October 9, 2025

I would say it's cultural appropriation but that is his culture so he's good.

— PSR (@PSRSurf) October 10, 2025

Lucifer, Bringer of Light.

— Brisology (@brisology) October 10, 2025

And, of course, one person had to reference the current season of “South Park,” where Trump is depicted having a same sex relationship with Satan.

It's the lovechild foretold in South Park 🤣🤣🤣 pic.twitter.com/FhEM9TxCEm

— Ken Akehurst (@ken_akehurst) October 10, 2025

Related...

  • OMFG: ‘South Park’ Goes Hard At Trump In X-Rated New Satanic Plot Twist

  • Nobel Chair Denies Deliberately Snubbing Trump On Peace Prize

  • 'Absolute Joke': MAGA Freaks Out Over Trump Nobel Peace Prize Snub

Read the original on HuffPost
data.summary: The picture caused one helluva reaction on social media.
data.author: HuffPost

data.title: First WA snow of the season to hit this week. Here's where
data.description:
KCPQ

First WA snow of the season to hit this week. Here's where

Will Wixey
October 9, 2025 at 3:29 PM·1 min read

The Brief

  • Washington's mountains may see snow this weekend, with elevations above 4,000 feet affected.

  • Areas like Stevens Pass could receive a dusting of snow, while lower elevations will experience fall rain.

  • FOX 13 Seattle is monitoring conditions as temperatures drop and snowfall begins.

STEVENS PASS, Wash. - Washington's mountains could see their first round of snow this week, forecasts project.

Timeline

Weather models are predicting snow could hit mountain passes as low as 4,500 feet from Sunday into Monday. That includes Stevens Pass, though Snoqualmie Pass will have to wait a bit longer.

Mountain peaks at elevations of 5,000 feet or higher could see between 3-6 inches of snow this weekend. That means several inches are possible in areas like the North Cascades Highway heading into next week.

Again, snowy conditions are only expected in the mountains, with lower elevation areas expected to see another round of fall rain. Lingering showers are expected in Seattle through Tuesday.

As temperatures start to cool down across western Washington, FOX 13 Seattle is tracking the latest conditions at the passes as the first round of snowfall arrives.

MORE NEWS FROM FOX 13 SEATTLE

Seattle mayor announces National Guard defense plan, issues executive orders

Man charged in WA State Capitol break-in, vandalism

Major delays expected on I-5, I-405 during busy Seattle sports weekend

Man shot in the face in South Seattle

Docs: Everett, WA man accused of setting fire to adult stores

To get the best local news, weather and sports in Seattle for free, sign up for the daily FOX Seattle Newsletter.

Download the free FOX LOCAL app for mobile in the Apple App Store or Google Play Store for live Seattle news, top stories, weather updates and more local and national news.

The Source

Information in this story comes from weather models interpreted by FOX 13 Seattle Chief Meteorologist Brian MacMillan and the National Weather Service.
data.summary: Washington's mountains may see their first round of snow this weekend, with elevations above 4,000 feet affected.
data.author: KCPQ

data.title: Bitcoin Crash Off the Table as Four-Year Cycle is Dead: Arthur Hayes
data.description:
CoinDesk

Bitcoin Crash Off the Table as Four-Year Cycle is Dead: Arthur Hayes

Omkar Godbole
October 8, 2025 at 11:34 PM·3 min read

Bitcoin (BTC) is unlikely to enter a bear market in the coming months as supportive monetary conditions are expected to prevail, effectively rendering the traditional four-year halving cycle obsolete, according to Arthur Hayes, chief investment officer and co-founder of Maelstrom.

In an essay titled "Long Live the King!" published Thursday, Arthur Hayes argued that the primary catalyst behind previous bitcoin bear markets in 2014, 2018, and 2022 was monetary tightening in major economies, not the four-year halving cycle. On each of these occasions, bitcoin’s price plunged by 70% to 80% from its bull market peak.

CoinDesk made a similar point in 2023, explaining that BTC's four-year bull-bear cycle centred around mining reward halvings is actually tied to fluctuations in fiat money supply and liquidity, rather than the halving events alone.

"As the four-year anniversary of this fourth cycle is upon us, traders wish to apply the historical pattern and forecast an end to this bull run,” Hayes wrote, explaining that the four-year cycle is dead and the impending fiat liquidity deluge will keep the bull market going.

The halving cycle

Halving refers to the programmed reduction in the per-block BTC emission every four years.

Since its inception in 2009, BTC has typically followed a roughly four-year cycle, characterized by a bull run leading up to and following the quadrennial reward halving, followed by an intense bear market that usually begins 16 to 18 months after the halving event.

The most recent, fourth Bitcoin halving occurred in April 2024. Hence, some market participants may be concerned that BTC's uptrend will soon peak, potentially paving the way for a year-long bear market.

This time is different

The ongoing bull market is likely to continue, invalidating the four-year cycle because monetary conditions are expected to remain accommodative, with money supply growth likely to accelerate rather than contract.

The U.S. government and its central bank are already in an easing mode and Japan could soon join the fray as the new PM is a big believer in the ultra-stimulatory Abenomics strategy.

"In the U.S., newly elected President Trump wants to run the economy hot. He routinely speaks about America growing in order to reduce its debt load," Hayes noted. "Trump also speaks about lowering the cost of housing to release trillions of dollars of trapped home equity because of the rapid rise in housing prices post-2008."

The Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 25 basis points to around 4% in September 2025 and is expected to implement further reductions totaling up to 100 basis points over the next 12 months, signaling a more accommodative monetary stance.

Lastly, Hayes noted that although China may not be as stimulatory as in previous Bitcoin bull runs, Beijing’s focus on ending deflation suggests it is unlikely to drain fiat liquidity, supporting continued price gains for BTC.

Hayes summed up this outlook by saying: "Listen to our monetary masters in Washington and Beijing. They clearly state that money shall be cheaper and more plentiful. Therefore, Bitcoin continues to rise in anticipation of this highly probable future."
data.summary: Arthur Hayes argues that Bitcoin’s traditional four-year market cycle has ended, as current shifts in global monetary policy indicate expanding fiat liquidity.
data.author: CoinDesk

data.title: Ocean City, state of Maryland urge residents to prep for worst with potent storm looming
data.description:
Delmarva Now | The Daily Times

Ocean City, state of Maryland urge residents to prep for worst with potent storm looming

Keith Demko and Olivia Minzola, Salisbury Daily Times
October 10, 2025 at 4:44 PM·4 min read

The town of Ocean and state of Maryland are preparing for the worst as a potent coastal storm is set to intensify off the Carolina coast on Oct. 10 and track northward, passing off the coast of Maryland this weekend.

Here's more on preparations, but first a link to the detailed forecast for the Lower Shore through Monday.

What's in weekend forecast for Salisbury, Ocean City?

WEEKEND FORECAST FOR SHORE: Coastal storm to hammer Maryland Lower Shore with flooding, high winds and more

Ocean City warns of possible king tides, dangerous surf conditions

The town of Ocean City also warned residents to be on high alert, and warned in particular of the following hazards:

  • High winds: Strong winds are expected to develop after midnight Saturday and continue into Sunday evening, with gusts of 50–55+ mph possible along the coast. Isolated power outages may occur.

  • King tides: Elevated tides throughout the weekend will exacerbate coastal flooding impacts.

  • Dangerous surf conditions: Hazardous surf and rip currents are expected at all beaches through the weekend.

To receive the latest updates, residents and visitors are encouraged to sign up for Ocean City’s e-news alerts at oceancitymd.gov/enews. Information and updates will also be shared through the following official channels:

  • Facebook: facebook.com/TownOfOceanCity

  • Twitter/X: @townofoceancity

  • Website: www.oceancitymd.gov/knowyourzone

  • FM Advisory Radio: 99.5

  • Town of Ocean City Access Channels: 4 & 15

Cruisin' announces date changes ahead of coastal flooding

Endless Summer Cruisin' event organizers have made a big change to this weekend's schedule for the Ocean City event.

Ahead of Sunday's storm, which is anticipated to bring coastal flooding and high winds, organizers have now moved all Sunday, Oct. 12, events to Saturday, Oct. 11. The news was shared in an Oct. 9 Facebook post.

"For the safety of our participants and spectators, we have made the decision to move all Sunday events to Saturday. Stay tuned for an updated, exciting, jam-packed Saturday schedule. Thank you for understanding," the post read.

ALL TO KNOW ON CRUISIN': Ocean City's Endless Summer Cruisin makes big schedule change with severe weather expected

Gov. Moore urges Marylanders to stay safe in storm

Gov. Wes Moore is also urging Marylanders to stay vigilant as the Maryland Department of Emergency Management is closely monitoring the storm. The state is preparing for a range of coastal and inland impacts, with the timing and severity dependent on the final storm track.

“We must take every available precaution to protect each other and keep our communities safe,” said Gov. Moore. “Our administration is continuing to watch this storm carefully as it develops, and we will communicate clearly with the public as the situation evolves. I also encourage all Marylanders to ensure their loved ones and neighbors are properly monitoring the potential for high winds, rain, and flooding. Take care of yourself. Take care of your people.”

The Maryland Department of Emergency Managementis coordinating closely with local jurisdictions and the National Weather Service, and advises those within the storm’s track to:

  • Avoid unnecessary travel during peak storm periods, particularly along vulnerable coastal and low-lying routes especially along the eastern seaboard.

  • Secure outdoor furniture, boats and other objects that may become projectiles in high wind.

  • Be prepared for temporary power outages — keep flashlights, batteries, and basic supplies handy.

  • Monitor local river gauges, and always avoid driving through flooded roadways.

  • Stay alert by following updates from your local emergency management office, NOAA/NWS forecasts, local media, and official briefings for updated information and possible watches or warnings.

Key impacts and risk expected in Maryland

  • Winds: Gusts up to 55 mph are possible along coastal zones and the Chesapeake Bay shoreline, with potential to extend into central Maryland.

  • Coastal and Tidal Flooding and Erosion: Moderate to major coastal flooding is expected on Sunday.

  • Wave heights through Sunday night may exceed 12-14 feet, with potentially significant beach erosion and wash-ups from waves.

  • Rain and dlooding: Several inches of rain late Saturday night into Sunday — possibly extending into Monday — may lead to small stream, creek, and river flooding, particularly in low-lying and poorly drained areas.

  • Power and infrastructure: The combination of wind and saturated soils raises the possibility of isolated power outages, tree damage, and downed lines.

  • Public safety and travel: Hazardous marine conditions, high surf, coastal flooding, and gusty winds will make some travel difficult — especially along coastal roads and bridges.

Residents are also encouraged to follow the Maryland Department of Emergency Management’s social media feeds:

  • X/Twitter: @MDMEMA

  • Facebook: facebook.com/MDMEMA

  • Bluesky: @mdmema.bsky.social

This article originally appeared on Salisbury Daily Times: Maryland, Ocean City warn residents of dangers of potent storm coming
data.summary: Ocean City and Maryland are preparing for the worst as a potent coastal storm is set to pass off the coast of Maryland this weekend.
data.author: Delmarva Now | The Daily Times

data.title: Three High School Football Players Killed In 'Horrific' Accident
data.description:
The Spun

Three High School Football Players Killed In 'Horrific' Accident

Chris Rosvoglou
October 10, 2025 at 8:20 AM·1 min read

Students and teachers from E.E. Smith High School in Fayetteville, North Carolina are mourning the loss of three students this week.

According to the Fayetteville Police Department, a one-vehicle accident occurred this Wednesday around 7:22 p.m. on Rosehill Road. The car veered off the road and crashed into a telephone pole. There were four people involved in this accident. The three students who went to E.E. Smith High School were declared dead at the scene. The fourth person was airlifted to the hospital.

Cumberland County Schools released a statement on this tragic accident.

”We are deeply saddened by the tragic loss of three E.E. Smith High School students,” Cumberland County Schools said, via WBTV. “Our thoughts and prayers are with their families, loved ones and the entire E.E. Smith Golden Bulls community during this difficult time.”

On Thursday night, it was reported by multiple outlets that Jai’hyon Elliott, Trevor Merritt and Nicholas Williams were the students involved in this fatal accident.

The school district announced that E.E. Smith High School's game against South View has been postponed. It was initially set for this Friday.

“The top priority of both schools continues to be providing support to students, faculty and staff as they process this tragedy,” Cumberland County Schools added. “We appreciate the kindness, understanding and support of both school communities during this difficult time.”

Our thoughts are with the victims' friends and family members.

This story was originally reported by The Spun on Oct 10, 2025, where it first appeared in the Trending section. Add The Spun as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

data.title: Trump Not Knowing How To Respond To A Simple Question Is Going Viral Because People Cannot Believe It's Real
data.description:
BuzzFeed

Trump Not Knowing How To Respond To A Simple Question Is Going Viral Because People Cannot Believe It's Real

Lee Moran
October 9, 2025 at 2:55 PM·4 min read

Donald Trump was asked at Wednesday’s White House roundtable that included far-right content creators ― and had Trump’s antifa bogeyman as the subject — if he’d “given any more thought to possibly suspending habeas corpus to not only deal with these insurrectionists across the nation but also to continue rapidly deporting illegal aliens.”

“Yeah, uh, suspending who?” the president replied.

The questioner repeated, “Uh, habeas corpus, sir.”

Q: Have you given any more thought to possibly suspending habeas corpus?TRUMP: Suspending who?Q: Habeas corpusTRUMP: I don't know. I'd rather leave that to Kristi. pic.twitter.com/DdjFnoUIrM

— Aaron Rupar (@atrupar) October 8, 2025

@atrupar

Related: I Cackled So Hard At These 18 Tweets About Trump Sending Troops To "War Ravaged Portland" That I Almost Forgot We're Living In A Dystopian Nightmare

“Oh, I don’t know. I’d rather leave that to Kristi. What do you think?” Trump said, punting the question to Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem.

“No, sir, I haven’t been a part of any discussions on that,” Noem replied.

On social media, critics mockingly suggested Trump thought he was being asked about suspending a person and not the constitutionally enshrined protection from unlawful detention.

“President Non Compos Mentis has no idea what the writ of habeas corpus is,” said conservative attorney George Conway.

President Non Compos Mentis has no idea what the writ of habeas corpus is. https://t.co/5xbFxxfoAF

— George Conway 👊🇺🇸🔥 (@gtconway3d) October 9, 2025

@georgeconway/ C-SPAN

Another commenter described the moment as “extremely hilarious if not so extremely dangerous.”

Q: “Are you going to suspend habeas corpus?”Trump: “Suspend who”This would be extremely hilarious if not so extremely dangerous.pic.twitter.com/oAryZOLO71

— Bryan Dawson🇺🇸 (@BryanDawsonUSA) October 9, 2025

@bryandawsonusa / C-SPAN/ @atrupar

Related: People Have NO Sympathy For This Trump Supporter Whose Pregnant Fiancé Was Denied A Visa

You can find more reactions below:

I guess Habeas Corpus must have been hangin' with those 2 Corinthians. https://t.co/cJHfNibvBp

— Michael Steele (@MichaelSteele) October 9, 2025

@atrupar / @michaelsteele

I don’t mean to be condescending to Trump or his supporters, but if MAGA wants to know, Habeas Corpus is Latin for “right to your body” and the guiding principle that are all afforded a fair trial. And by “condescending” I mean talk down to. https://t.co/qFgIbCHHQR

— CALL TO ACTIVISM (@CalltoActivism) October 8, 2025

@calltoactivism/ @atrupar/ C-SPAN

Habeas corpus is a basic pillar of American law. Passing the question to Kristi Noem is not leadership. It’s outsourcing your civics homework to someone else and hoping no one notices.

— Chetter 📢🗽⚖💙 Beacon for Democracy (@ChetterHub) October 8, 2025

@chetterhub

I’m pretty sure if Trump finds out who Habeas Corpus is, he’ll do everything in his power to try to deport him, given his Latin roots. pic.twitter.com/LJOocSBZkr

— Canada Hates Trump (@AntiTrumpCanada) October 9, 2025

@antitrumpcanada/ C-SPAN

Related: John Oliver Says The Only Way To Stop "Weak Bully" Trump Is With This 4-Word Phrase He Definitely Doesn't Want To Hear

He doesn’t know what it is.“Who?” https://t.co/6C5liQd2uB

— Mehdi Hasan (@mehdirhasan) October 8, 2025

@mehdirhanse/ C-SPAN

My guy fobbed it off to the next person cos he didn’t have a clue 🤣 https://t.co/nnlNljSr7y

— Wale Gates (@walegates) October 9, 2025

@walegates/ C-SPAN

He knows practically nothing about the law. But that’s ok, because in his mind and in MAGA world, the law is whatever he says it is at any given moment. https://t.co/DUX1ACecm0

— Jorge K Gonzalez (@jorgekgonzalez) October 8, 2025

@jorgekgonzalez

Neither one of them has any idea what that means nor who can suspend it… https://t.co/EgtF2P8hW8

— Martina Navratilova (@Martina) October 8, 2025

@martina

Noem thinks habeas corpus is the president’s constitutional right to remove people from the country.Trump? Thinks Habeas is a person. https://t.co/E5LGF7NIJb

— David Pepper (@DavidPepper) October 9, 2025

@davidpepper

We’d rather not leave that to Kristi — she doesn’t know what Habeas corpus is either pic.twitter.com/Zu9XuCrmhV https://t.co/tH4OxoUmn5

— House Homeland Security Committee Democrats (@HomelandDems) October 8, 2025

@homelanddems/ C-SPAN/@atrupar

Habeas Corpus? Dont really know him. He was just the coffee boy. pic.twitter.com/j0cYkc4w2O

— B̴o̴r̴i̴s̴ Trump (@BorisTrump6) October 9, 2025

@boristrump6 / C-SPAN

Comforting. https://t.co/kK5Ls5gYxW

— Gavin Newsom (@GavinNewsom) October 8, 2025

@gavinnewsom/ @atrupar/ C-SPAN

Kamala Harris was an option. Y’all chose this bullshit. https://t.co/aeRLN0c2Rx

— Warren (@swd2) October 8, 2025

@swd2 @atrupar/ C-SPAN

Trump is thinking about suspending Habeas Corpus to keep him from performing the halftime show at the Super Bowl.

— Ron Filipkowski (@RonFilipkowski) October 8, 2025

@ronfilipowski

data.title: The latest on Mark Sanchez after former QB pleads not guilty, alleged victim released from hospital
data.description:
Yahoo Sports

The latest on Mark Sanchez after former QB pleads not guilty, alleged victim released from hospital

Jack Baer
·Staff writer
Updated October 9, 2025 at 4:02 PM·5 min read

One of the most bizarre stories of the year has continued to unfold this week, as potential repercussions mount for former NFL quarterback Mark Sanchez after an incident that saw him stabbed multiple times, then criminally charged in Indianapolis.

The allegations against Sanchez revolve around an incident in which police claim he confronted a truck driver on the job, became aggressive and then got pepper-sprayed and stabbed after things got physical, with the victim saying Sanchez smelled of alcohol. That account is reportedly backed up by the alleged victim's statement, security footage from nearby hotels and a witness.

Sanchez was initially charged with battery resulting in injury, public intoxication, and unlawful entry of a motor vehicle — all misdemeanors. However, prosecutors added a Level 5 felony battery charge on Monday, which carries a one-to-six year sentence and up to a $10,000 fine.

Mark Sanchez pleads not guilty through lawyer

Updates on Sanchez's health have so far been slow, but he appears to still be hospitalized in Indianapolis after sustaining several stab wounds to his torso. While Sanchez has not been physically booked in jail, the legal process is still playing out, with his lawyer waiving a pretrial hearing and entering a not guilty plea on Thursday.

That move appears procedural — if Sanchez were to plead guilty, it would be after a discussion with prosecution he doesn't seem capable of right now. His next court date is Nov. 4.

Meanwhile, the only communication from the Sanchez camp is a statement released earlier this week:

"This has been a deeply distressing time for everyone involved," Mark's brother, Nick Sanchez said, via Fox59's Angela Ganote. "Mark and our family are incredibly grateful for the concern, love, and support we’ve received over the past few days. Mark remains under medical care for the serious injuries he sustained and is focused on his recovery as the legal process continues. We would like to extend our heartfelt thanks to the first responders and medical staff.”

Mark Sanchez to miss this weekend's NFL broadcast

With Sanchez in the hospital and facing potential prison time, it's not a surprise his employer isn't planning to use him anytime soon.

Fox Sports announced its broadcast assignments for the week Thursday with Sanchez absent. His play-by-play partner of the past couple weeks, Chris Myers, will instead work with Mark Schlereth in the booth. Brady Quinn filled in for Sanchez alongside Myers last week.

The NFL's biggest stars are on @NFLonFOX for a weekend of top-tier matchups ⭐️

Tune in Sunday at 11 AM ET on FOX ⤵️ pic.twitter.com/gp9qjR8LZE

— FOX Sports PR (@FOXSportsPR) October 9, 2025

Thanks to bye weeks, Fox probably won't need Sanchez for a while. Awful Announcing notes it has a maximum of five games scheduled every week until Nov. 9, so Fox can roll with their current teams without having to figure out another substitution.

Of course, the bigger question is if Sanchez returns to Fox at all. Alleged victim Perry Tole has reportedly filed a lawsuit against both Sanchez and his employer, and networks have cut broadcasters for less severe falls in public image. It seems likely Fox will want more of the legal process to play out before making a final decision, though.

Prosecutor says Mark Sanchez charges could be further upgraded

While Sanchez faces a serious charge that could land him in prison, he might even have more to deal with as Tole's condition is evaluated and authorities look into the incident.

Marion County prosecutor Ryan Mears indicated in an interview with Good Morning America that the felony battery charge could be further upgraded, with GMA noting an aggravated battery charge would carry a three-to-16 year prison sentence if convicted.

Mears' comment, via GMA:

"For us, it's all about gathering as much information as possible. Very often times, that comes from medical providers being able to say, 'As a result of these injuries, this person is going to have to deal with the following things for the rest of their life or a significant period of their life."

On that note, the 69-year-old Tole's attorney has already said his client is "gonna have scars forever" after sustaining a large gash on his face.

Alleged Mark Sanchez victim released from hospital, unable to attend son's wedding

Tole's attorney said Tuesday his client has been released from the hospital and is recovering at home, but his injuries remain serious, via Fox 59:

”What you don’t see in those pictures is that the inside of his mouth and his tongue have been affected in a way that makes it difficult for him to speak, so he has a very hard time communicating right now and that has a lot to do with his injuries, exhaustion,” said May. “He’s a tough son of a gun. It's remarkable that he’s still alive.”

Unfortunately, the injuries also mean Tole will be unable to attend his son's wedding, another lawyer said via ABC News:

“Mentally, what he and his wife are going through has been considerable," May said. "He’s just really shaken up."

Tole’s son is getting married next week and "he’s not gonna be able to attend the wedding, so he’s sad about that," victim attorney Eddie Reichert added.

Tole reportedly ran into Sanchez while performing his job as a truck driver for a company that recycles and disposes of used cooking oil. Sanchez reportedly wandered up to him past midnight and claimed the manager of the hotel where Tole was performing his job said he couldn't be there.

On Wednesday, Tole's company Automatic Commercial and Restaurant Cooking Oil Management released a statement to IndyStar promising support for its employee:

In a statement to IndyStar, the company extended its "heartfelt thanks" to the local first responders "for providing exceptional assistance in responding to the incident, helping our employee reach safety, and get medical care."

The company said it had reached out to Tole and his family to "offer support and [the company] will be determining what is needed in the days and weeks to come."
data.summary: Marion County prosecutor Ryan Mears indicated the charges Sanchez faces could be upgraded again.
data.author: Yahoo Sports
